assumptions: Please act as a professional Verilog designer.
Implement a module of a 3-bit comparator for comparing binary numbers.
A [2:0]: First 3-bit input operand (the first binary number to compare).
B [2:0]: Second 3-bit input operand (the second binary number to compare).
Comparison Logic: The module compares the two 3-bit binary numbers A and B using combinational logic.
The outputs A_greater, A_equal, and A_less are determined based on the comparison of A and B.
A_greater is set to 1 if A > B, A_equal is set to 1 if A == B, and A_less is set to 1 if A < B.
Output Encoding: The outputs are mutually exclusive, meaning only one of the three outputs will be high (1) at any given time, based on the comparison results.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
A_greater is high iff A > B
A_equal is high iff A == B
A_less is high iff A < B
Exactly one of the three outputs is high for any legal input combination
cases.goal: Exercise representative legal input combinations and verify exclusive output encoding.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Adds explicit exclusivity check to the original basic case.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['A', 'B']
Apply a small set of distinct input pairs covering all three comparison outcomes.
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Check outputs after inputs have settled; no sequential timing is required.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Boundary values (0 and 7) produce correct comparison results.
Zero‑like patterns (e.g., 3'b000) and one‑like patterns (e.g., 3'b111) are handled correctly.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary‑value and width‑sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Reinforces that the comparator works for extreme operand values.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['A', 'B']
Drive A and B with min/max values and verify output mapping.
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe outputs after each stimulus stabilizes; no internal state is assumed.

expected_properties: When inputs A and B are swapped, the relationship outputs invert accordingly: A_greater ↔ A_less, A_equal remains unchanged.
cases.goal: Validate that the comparator's output mapping is symmetric with respect to input swapping.
notes: Ensures mutual exclusivity and correct logical inversion without assuming any timing latency.
preconditions: Both A and B are driven with legal 3‑bit values.
stimulus_intent: Drive a pair of distinct values (e.g., A=3'b101, B=3'b011) then swap them and compare outputs.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after each input vector stabilizes; no fixed-cycle latency is assumed.
